rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike, Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Seal Document
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 30, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC's Motion To Strike, Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Seal Document READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Nationstar Mortgage LLC's Motion to Strike or Seal is DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Duke Partners, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 30, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Duke Partners, LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Defendant Duke Partners, LLC met their burden of demonstrating that there is no triable issue of material fact. Moving party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Nationstar Mortgage Llc And Wells Fargo Bank, National Association As Trustees Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 30, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANTs NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC, and WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants Nationstar and Wells Fargo's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to the first (breach of contract), second (negligence), third (wrongful foreclosure), and fifth (cancellation of deed) causes of action as Defendants met their burden of showing Plaintiffs' claims lack merit. Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to the fourth cause of action for Violation of Bus. & Prof. Code § 17200 because Defendant Nationstar failed to demonstrate that Plaintiffs' claim lacks merit. Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)
